content.problemStatement:
You are given a binary string SSS.

In one second, the following scenario happens **simultaneously** and **independently** for **all** the bits which are set to 111 in the string:


Change the bit from 111 to 000.
If the left neighbour exists and is 000, change it to 111.
If the right neighbour exists and is 000, change it to 111.
For example, if S=010S = 010S=010 initially, then after 111 second, S=101S = 101S=101 (the 111 bit and both its neighbours were changed). After another second, S=010S = 010S=010. Here, the first and the last bit were changed to 000 because earlier they were 111. The middle bit was changed because it was 000 earlier and it was a neighbour of a 111 bit.

Find out the string SSS after KKK seconds.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of string SSS and the number of seconds.
The next line describes the string SSS.




Output Format
For each test case, output the string SSS after exactly KKK seconds.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10610^6106.
SSS can only contain the characters 000 or 111.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The middle bit is changed to 111 since it had a neighbouring set bit (in this case both left and right) and both the set bits are changed to 000. Hence, after one second, it is 101101101.
Test case 222: After first second, the string SSS will be 010100101001010. After another second , the string becomes 101011010110101.
